rationale: In the citing paper, Iyer and Villas-Boas (2003) appears mainly as part of a grouped background citation on powerful retailers and channel research. The paper does not build its core model, equilibrium derivations, or propositions on that bargaining framework; instead it uses a take-it-or-leave-it contract structure. A later mention cites it only as an example of how manufacturer bargaining power could be incorporated in future extensions, which is peripheral rather than a functional basis for the main analysis.
